user/testbin/testfile/testfile.c

Go to the documentation of this file.
00001 /*
00002  * testopen.c
00003  *
00004  *  Created on: May 16, 2010
00005  *      Author: Luigi Grimaudo  153740
00006  *                      Piera Gueli             160119
00007  */
00008 
00009 #include <stdio.h>
00010 #include <string.h>
00011 #include <unistd.h>
00012 #include <err.h>
00013 
00014 int
00015 main(int argc, char *argv[])
00016 {
00017         char writebuf[11] = "Hello\nworld";
00018         static char readbuf[12];
00019         static char readbuf1[12];
00020         static char readbuf2[10];
00021 
00022         int fd, rv,fd2;
00023         off_t c=0;
00024         rv=0;
00025 
00026         if (argc!=3 && writebuf!=NULL) {
00027                 printf("Error usage: filetest <filename> %s or fileridirect <filename> %s", argv[1],argv[2]);
00028                 return -1;
00029         }
00030         
00031         printf("\n****** Program to test file system call *****************\n");
00032 
00033 
00034         printf("\n********************** Open *****************************\n");
00035         printf("Trying to open %s\n", argv[1]);
00036 
00037         fd = open(argv[1], O_RDWR);
00038 
00039         if (fd<0) {
00040                 printf("Open error %d...\n", fd);
00041                 return -1;
00042         }
00043         else
00044         {
00045                 printf("Open successful...\n");
00046                 printf("File opened with file descriptor = %d\n", fd);
00047         }
00048 
00049         printf("********************* End Open **************************\n");
00050 
00051         printf("\n********************** Write ****************************\n");
00052         printf("Trying to write on %s\n", argv[1]);
00053 
00054         rv = write(fd, writebuf, 11);
00055         if (rv<0) {
00056                 printf("Write error %d...\n", rv);
00057         }
00058         else {
00059                 printf("Write successful...\n");
00060                 printf("Written %d bytes on %s\n", rv, argv[1]);
00061         }
00062 
00063         printf("********************* End Write *************************\n");
00064 
00065         printf("\n********************** Close ****************************\n");
00066         printf("Trying to close on %s\n", argv[1]);
00067 
00068         rv = close(fd);
00069         if (rv<0) {
00070                 printf("Close error %d...\n", rv);
00071         }
00072         else {
00073                 printf("Close successful...\n");
00074                 printf("File closed %s\n", argv[1]);
00075         }
00076 
00077         printf("********************* End Close *************************\n");
00078 
00079         printf("\n********************** Re-Write *************************\n");
00080         printf("Trying to re-write on %s\n", argv[1]);
00081 
00082         rv = write(fd, writebuf, 11);
00083         if (rv<0) {
00084                 printf("Re-Write error %d...\n", rv);
00085         }
00086         else {
00087                 printf("Re-Write successful...\n");
00088                 printf("Re-Written %d bytes on %s\n", rv, argv[1]);
00089         }
00090 
00091         printf("********************* End Re-Write **********************\n");
00092 
00093         printf("\n********************** Re-Open  *************************\n");
00094         printf("Trying to re-open  %s\n", argv[1]);
00095 
00096         fd = open(argv[1], O_RDWR);
00097 
00098         if (fd<0) 
00099         {
00100                 printf("Open for read error %d...\n", fd);
00101                 return -1;
00102         }
00103         else
00104         {
00105                 printf("Re-Open successful...\n");
00106                 printf("File re-opened with file descriptor = %d\n", fd);
00107         }
00108 
00109         printf("********************** End Open For Read ****************\n");
00110 
00111         printf("\n********************* Read ******************************\n");
00112         
00113         rv = read(fd, readbuf, 12);
00114         readbuf[12] = 0;
00115         if (rv<0) {
00116                 printf("Read error %d...\n", rv);
00117         }
00118         else {
00119                 printf("Read successful...\n");
00120                 printf("Read %d bytes on %s: \n%s\n", rv, argv[1], readbuf);
00121         }
00122 
00123         printf("********************* End Read **************************\n");
00124         
00125 
00126         printf("\n********************** Lseek ****************************\n");
00127         rv = lseek(fd,c,SEEK_SET);
00128         
00129         if (rv<0) {
00130                 printf("Lseek modalità SEEK_SET con pos = %d error %d...\n", (int)c,rv);
00131         }
00132         else {
00133                 printf("Lseek modalità SEEK_SET con pos = %d successful...\n",(int)c);
00134         }
00135         
00136         printf("********************** End Lseek ************************\n");
00137 
00138         printf("\n********************* Read ******************************\n");
00139         rv = read(fd, readbuf1, 12);
00140         readbuf1 [12]=  0;
00141         if (rv<0) {
00142                 printf("Read error %d...\n", rv);
00143         }
00144         else {
00145                 printf("Read successful...\n");
00146                 printf("Read %d bytes on %s: \n%s\n", rv, argv[1], readbuf1);
00147         }
00148 
00149         printf("********************* End Read **************************\n");
00150 
00151 
00152         printf("\n********************** Lseek ****************************\n");
00153         c = -6;
00154         rv = lseek(fd,c,SEEK_CUR);
00155         
00156         if (rv<0) {
00157                 printf("Lseek modalità SEEK_CUR con pos = %d error %d...\n",(int)c,rv);
00158         }
00159         else {
00160                 printf("Lseek modalità SEEK_CUR con pos = %d successful...\n",(int)c);
00161         }
00162         
00163         printf("********************** End Lseek ************************\n");
00164 
00165 
00166         printf("\n********************** Re-Write *************************\n");
00167         printf("Trying to re-write on %s\n", argv[1]);
00168 
00169         rv = write(fd, "\nCiao mondo", 11);
00170         if (rv<0) {
00171                 printf("Re-Write error %d...\n", rv);
00172         }
00173         else {
00174                 printf("Re-Write successful...\n");
00175                 printf("Re-Written %d bytes on %s\n", rv, argv[1]);
00176         }
00177 
00178         printf("********************* End Re-Write **********************\n");
00179 
00180 
00181         printf("\n********************** Lseek ****************************\n");
00182         c = -5;
00183         rv = lseek(fd,c,SEEK_END);
00184         
00185         if (rv<0) {
00186                 printf("Lseek modalità SEEK_END con pos = %d error %d...\n", (int)c,rv);
00187         }
00188         else {
00189                 printf("Lseek modalità SEEK_END con pos = %d successful...\n",(int)c);
00190         }
00191         
00192         printf("********************** End Lseek ************************\n");
00193 
00194         printf("\n********************* Read ******************************\n");
00195         
00196         rv = read(fd, readbuf2, 40);
00197         readbuf2 [10]=  0;
00198         if (rv<0) {
00199                 printf("Read error %d...\n", rv);
00200         }
00201         else {
00202                 printf("Read successful...\n");
00203                 printf("Read %d bytes on %s: \n%s\n", rv, argv[1], readbuf2);
00204         }
00205 
00206         printf("********************* End Read **************************\n");
00207         
00208 
00209         printf("\n********************** Open *****************************\n");
00210         printf("Trying to open %s\n", argv[1]);
00211 
00212         fd2 = open(argv[2], O_RDWR);
00213 
00214         if (fd2<0) {
00215                 printf("Open error %d...\n", fd2);
00216                 return -1;
00217         }
00218         else
00219         {
00220                 printf("Open successful...\n");
00221                 printf("File opened with file descriptor = %d\n", fd2);
00222         }
00223 
00224         printf("********************* End Open **************************\n");
00225 
00226         printf("\n********************** Dup2 *****************************\n");
00227         printf("Trying to dup %s\n", argv[2]);
00228 
00229         fd2 = dup2(fd2, 1);
00230 
00231         if (fd2<0) {
00232                 printf("Dup2 error %d...\n", fd);
00233                 return -1;
00234         }
00235         else
00236         {
00237                 printf("Dup2 successful...\n");
00238                 printf("File dup_opened with file descriptor = %d\n", fd2);
00239         }
00240         
00241         printf("********************* End Dup2 **************************\n");
00242 
00243         
00244         printf("\n********************** Close ****************************\n");
00245         printf("Trying to close on %s\n", argv[1]);
00246 
00247         rv = close(fd);
00248         if (rv<0) {
00249                 printf("Close error %d...\n", rv);
00250         }
00251         else {
00252                 printf("Close successful...\n");
00253                 printf("File closed %s\n", argv[1]);
00254         }
00255 
00256         printf("********************* End Close *************************\n");
00257 
00258 
00259         printf("\n********************** Close ****************************\n");
00260         printf("Trying to close on %s\n", argv[2]);
00261 
00262         rv = close(fd2);
00263         if (rv<0) {
00264                 printf("Close error %d...\n", rv);
00265         }
00266         
00267 
00268         printf("********************* End Close *************************\n");
00269 
00270         printf("Passed filetest.\n");
00271 
00272         return 0;
00273 }
00274
